title: CDNs are the internet copy machine
hook: A CDN makes the web feel close by putting copies near people. The hard part is deciding when those copies stop being true.

blocks.html: If every image, script, and page has to travel from one origin server, users far away pay for geography. A CDN stores copies at edge locations so repeated requests take a shorter path.

blocks.title: curl · edge hit
blocks.caption: The useful signal is usually already there.

beats.heading: Version the things you can cache forever

blocks.html: Static assets love long cache lifetimes when the filename changes with the content. User-specific pages need stricter rules.

blocks.code:
app.8f3a91.css
kit.42aa10.js

# new content, new filename, old cache stays safe
blocks.caption: Hashed filenames turn cache invalidation into deployment math.

blocks.html: CDN bugs usually show up in headers: cache status, age, vary, and cache-control. Read those before blaming the app.

items.label: origin shielded by many edge copies
private user data in public cache

beats.heading: The edge is part of the app

blocks.html: A CDN is not just hosting plumbing. It is where performance, correctness, and cache policy meet.
